Muscles of mastication are predominantly supplied by the maxillary artery. Specifically, the second part of the maxillary artery provides several branches that serve the various muscles of mastication. Therefore,
\boxed{B} Based on the detailed breakdown of the anatomy and blood supply pathways described in the articles, particularly focusing on the second part of the maxillary artery providing key branches like the anterior, middle, and posterior deep temporal arteries which specifically supply the temporalis muscle, and considering additional contributions from related branches like those serving the masseter, medial, and lateral pterygoids—these consistently align with option B being correct.
